routed_to_human.note: Reviewed (skeptic/methodologist/claims-auditor): comparative claim 'best 34% at DMF/24h' matches Table 1 (max of {15,19,19,34}=34 at entry 6). No over-reach; T2 prose-vs-table comparison has no numeric verifier in the registry yet.
Reviewed: 'syn/anti up to 1:69 at Table 3 entry 3' matches the extracted table (entry 3 dr=1:69 is the max anti ratio). No over-reach; no numeric dr-extremum verifier exists yet.

Reviewed (domain-expert/methodologist): DFT activation barriers 7.1 (gas) / 6.7 (DMSO) kcal/mol reproduce Table 4 exactly; magnitudes are physically reasonable for an organocatalytic aldol TS. No numeric DFT-energy verifier covers it yet.

Reviewed: intermediate energies +0.4 (gas, endothermic) / -2.3 (DMSO) kcal/mol reproduce Table 4 / Figure 3; sign and solvent-stabilization narrative are internally consistent. No numeric verifier covers it yet.
Reviewed: 'up to 90% with 4-iodophenol (Table 6 entry 3)' matches the extracted table (max Heck yield=90 at entry 3, cat 4). No over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet.
Reviewed: 'MW yields significantly higher than reflux' supported by Table 6 same-substrate pair (4-OH, cat 3: reflux 43% vs MW 81%). Comparative claim holds; no numeric verifier covers it yet.

Abstract framing vs Table 3 ((S)-4): (a) the '80–95%' yield range understates the actual spread — tabulated (S)-4 yields run 59–98% (entries 8,11,13 fall below 80; entries 5,10 reach 91–98), so '80–95%' is neither the min nor the max. (b) 'ee greater than 99%' rests on qualitative '>99' cells (entries 1,3,7); the highest *numeric* ee in the table is 98%. Defensible as a best-case bound but the abstract reads as a typical-case range. Confirm by reconciling the abstract range against the full Table 3 column once a T2 prose-vs-table extremum verifier exists.
routed_to_human.quote: achieving yields of 80–95%, excellent diastereoselectivities (1:69 syn/anti), and enantiomeric excesses greater than 99%

routed_to_human.note: Over-reach against the cited entries themselves: for {1,3,5,7,9,10} the bound 'yield > 85%' fails at entry 3 (84%), and 'ee > 95%' fails at entry 5 (92%). The other cited entries satisfy both (ee cells are '>99' or 98%). Tight, two-entry boundary violation — surfaced as advisory, not a hard flag, because no calibrated T2/T4 prose-vs-table verifier is admitted yet and I must not manufacture a deterministic verdict. Confirm by checking each cited entry against the bound.
routed_to_human.quote: achieving yields above 85% and enantiomeric excesses greater than 95% (Table 3, entries 1, 3, 5, 7, 9 and 10)

routed_to_human.note: Evidence-binding gap (T4): Table 5 reports only yields for the MBH adducts — no ee, dr, or chiral-HPLC column. The 'racemic in all cases' assertion is therefore not supported by the bound evidence; it needs the chiral analysis (ee≈0 or HPLC traces showing 1:1 enantiomers) to be checkable. Confirm by locating the MBH ee data; if absent, the claim is unsubstantiated.
routed_to_human.quote: each MBH adduct was obtained as a racemic mixture in all cases

routed_to_human.note: Same qualitative-bound caveat as the abstract (c1): the Conclusions' 'ee > 99%' is carried by Table 3 '>99' cells (entries 1,3,7); the largest numeric ee reported is 98%. Internally consistent with the table as a ceiling, but a reader should know '>99%' is a reported bound on a subset, not a measured maximum across all substrates.

EDS line check (T1 external-knowledge): Cl Kα at 2.622 keV is correct (tabulated Cl Kα1 = 2.622 keV). The Pd line quoted at 3.382 keV does NOT match any tabulated Pd emission line — Pd Lα1≈2.84, Lβ1≈2.99, Lβ2≈3.17, Kα≈21.18 keV; the nearest (Lβ2) is ~0.21 keV away. m/z 289/307 (FAB+) are plausible Pd-Lys fragments but not recomputed here. Confirm the Pd Kα/L assignment against the actual EDS spectrum; the energy as transcribed is anomalous. No emission-line verifier exists yet.
routed_to_human.quote: the presence of chlorine atoms, detected in the EDS spectrum (Kα emission) at 2.622 keV, and of palladium at 3.382 keV

evidence.quote:
List reported the synthesis
of aromatic aldol adducts in good yields and enantiomeric excesses approaching 80%, by
catalyzing the reactions with L-proline (30 mol%)
